name: Maple Apple Pie
recipeInstructions:
7 cups Apples, peeled and sliced
3/4 cup Maple syrup
2 tablespoons Cornstarch
1/2 cup Chopped pecans
2 tablespoons Maple syrup
1 teaspoon Butter
7 to 8 Tablespoons Cold water
Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
For the crust, combine flour, baking powder, and salt in a large mixing bowl. Cut in Crisco shortening with a pastry blender or two knives until the mixture is uniform. Sprinkle cold water in, 1 tablespoon at a time, until dough forms a ball. Divide dough into 2 equal parts.
On lightly floured surface, roll bottom crust into a circle that is 1/8 inch thick and 1/2 inch larger than a 9-inch pie plate. Gently ease crust into the greased plate, being careful not to stretch the dough. Trim the edge even with the plate.
For the filling, microwave apple slices on high for 2 minutes. Stir; repeat procedure twice or until apples begin to soften. Set aside.
In a separate microwaving bowl, combine maple syrup and cornstarch. Microwave for 2 minutes; stir. Repeat procedure until the mixture starts to thicken. Add the thickened mixture to apples. Toss lightly until apples are well coated; set aside to cool.
Spoon cooled apple filling into the unbaked pastry shell.
For the nut filling, combine pecans, maple syrup, and butter in a microwaving bowl. Microwave on high for 1 minute, stirring, then continue to microwave for an additional minute on medium setting. Stir to break up the mixture. Spread nut mixture on top of apple filling.
On lightly floured surface, roll out top crust the same as bottom.
Carefully lift top crust onto filled pie and trim 1/2 inch beyond the edge of the pie plate. Fold top edge under the crust. Crimp edges or flute as desired. Slit top crust with a knife to allow steam to escape.
Bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 60 minutes, or until apples start to bubble. Brush the top of the pie with maple syrup and continue to bake an additional 5 minutes.
